summary: Justin Brennan discusses personal development, relationships, masculine/feminine dynamics, therapy, and life philosophy in a mentorship conversation with Brian Norton. The content focuses on emotional intelligence, attachment styles, and finding fulfillment through philanthropy rather than money alone. No multifamily deal analysis, underwriting, sourcing, or market-specific strategies are presented.
takeaways: When your spouse shares emotions, ask 'Do you want my voice or my ears right now?' - your job is to hold space, not solve problems
Men need: supportive spouse, peace in home, physical intimacy. Women need: feeling provided for/protected, emotional safety, emotional intimacy
The 'shoreline' analogy: be stable like a shoreline while waves (her emotions) come and go at different heights
Four attachment styles determine relationship patterns: secure, anxious, avoidant, fearful-avoidant - mostly formed in childhood
Hypnotic reprogramming in meditative state can reframe traumatic memories and break limiting beliefs planted in subconscious
Never keep more than 6 months cash reserves in US bank accounts - diversify into tangible assets (real estate, crypto stablecoins, treasuries) to hedge currency devaluation
The $10,000 check accountability mechanism: pain/fear drives decisions more than pleasure - use external commitment to force execution
title: What I Wish Rich People Knew
